-\medskip\noindent\textbf{The boundary-bulk reconstruction thesis.}
-\emph{The boundary chiral algebra~$\cA$ determines its bulk via the
-chiral derived centre~$\cZ^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$; the
-modular characteristic~$\kappa(\cA)$ is the holographic central charge;
-the shadow obstruction tower~$\{S_r(\cA)\}_{r \ge 2}$ is the holographic
-correction hierarchy; and the Drinfeld double
-$U_\cA = \cA \bowtie \cA^!$ is the universal boundary-bulk algebra.}
-The bar complex computes the boundary $\mapsto$ bulk transform; the
-cobar inverts it on the Koszul locus.
-Each theorem isolates one structural property of this transform.
-Theorem~A constructs the bar-cobar adjunction and its Verdier intertwining
-(Definition~\ref{def:chiral-koszul-pair}).
-Theorem~B inverts the transform: at genus~$0$ via chiral
-Koszulity (Theorem~\ref{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}),
-at genus~$g \ge 1$ via the inductive genus step.
-Theorem~C constrains the Koszul dual pair by a family-specific sum rule:
-$\kappa(\cA) + \kappa(\cA^!) = K_\cA$, where $K = 0$ for affine
-Kac--Moody and free fields and $K = 13$ for Virasoro
-(self-dual at $c = 13$).
-Theorem~D identifies $\kappa$
-as the one-loop coefficient of the bulk partition
-function on the uniform-weight lane; the multi-weight
-correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$ at $g \ge 2$ is a
-separate higher-loop contribution.
-Theorem~H bounds the bulk state space: at generic level on the
-Koszul locus, $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ has total dimension at most four.
+\medskip\noindent\textbf{The forced chain A $\Rightarrow$ B $\Rightarrow$ C $\Rightarrow$ D $\Rightarrow$ H.}
+Without Theorem~A, the dual object is only a formal symbol: there is
+no theorem identifying
+$\mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}\barB_X(\cA)$ with a chiral algebra.
+Without Theorem~B, the bar construction can lose the boundary algebra.
+Without Theorem~C, higher-genus deformation theory has no canonical
+polarization. Without Theorem~D, the genus tower has no distinguished
+scalar shadow. Without Theorem~H, the bulk coefficient ring is
+uncontrolled. The unique survivor of all five failures is the
+genus-completed bar differential, first in the ordered $E_1$ theory and
+then in its $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant shadow.
+
+Theorem~A identifies the homotopy Koszul dual on the quadratic Koszul
+lane: for a chiral Koszul pair, the unit and counit are
+quasi-isomorphisms and Verdier duality exchanges the two members of the
+pair. Theorem~B shows that the bar-cobar transform is faithful on the
+Koszul locus: genus~$0$ is unconditional, while the genus-$g \ge 1$
+inversion is proved on the modular pre-Koszul lane and is unconditional
+on the standard CFT-type landscape except integer-spin $\beta\gamma$.
+Once both sides are present, Theorem~C forces the Verdier decomposition
+$\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)$ of the ambient complex;
+the Lagrangian splitting is proved, while the ambient
+shifted-symplectic upgrade remains conditional on perfectness and
+nondegeneracy. Tracing the same object yields Theorem~D: on the
+uniform-weight modular Koszul lane,
+$\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$ at all genera and the free
+energies are the $\hat A$-series; at genus~$1$ this scalar statement is
+unconditional for every family, while at genus $g \ge 2$ multi-weight
+algebras acquire the explicit cross-channel term
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$. Theorem~H identifies the coefficient
+ring of the same universal class: on the Koszul locus,
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n \notin \{0,1,2\}$ and
+\[
+P_\cA(t)
+=
+\dim Z(\cA)
++ \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
++ \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.
+\]
+For Heisenberg this gives $P(t) = 1+t+t^2$; for generic Virasoro and
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras it gives $P(t) = 1+t^2$; for generic
+affine Kac--Moody one has
+$\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\mathfrak g)) \cong \mathfrak g$, so the coefficient
+ring is still polynomial but need not be four-dimensional.
 
 \medskip\noindent
 All five theorems are $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant projections of a single

 \noindent\textbf{MC1} (PBW concentration): Bar cohomology of every standard-landscape chiral algebra concentrates in PBW degrees, proved for all standard families by explicit computation.
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): $\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$ exists for every modular Koszul chiral algebra by the bar-intrinsic construction (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}): $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$. The shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of its finite-order projections. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$ (Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}).
+\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): the symmetric, coinvariant statement is
+$\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$, proved by
+the bar-intrinsic construction
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}\textup{)}:
+$\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is Maurer--Cartan because
+$D_\cA^2 = 0$. The primitive ordered statement is
+$\Theta_\cA^{\Eone} := D_\cA^{\Eone} - \dzero \in
+\MC({\gAmod}^{\Eone})$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-mc-element}\textup{)}, and averaging
+recovers~$\Theta_\cA$. The shadow obstruction tower
+$\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of the finite-order projections of this
+coinvariant class. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element
+collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$
+\textup{(}Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}\textup{)}.
 
 \noindent\textbf{MC3} (Thick generation): Proved for all simple types on the evaluation-generated core via multiplicity-free $\ell$-weights~\cite{ChariMoura06} (Theorem~\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}), with DK comparison proved separately (Corollary~\ref{cor:dk23-all-types}, Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types}). For type~$A$, shifted-prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are also proved (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}). DK-4/5 (extension beyond evaluation modules) is downstream.
 
  The proof decomposes into three layers: MC3a (evaluation core, all types, unconditional), MC3b (categorical thick generation, all types, unconditional), and MC3c (full category~$\mathcal{O}$, type~$A$ unconditional; other types conditional on Conjecture~\ref{conj:rank-independence-step2}, the rank-independence lift-and-lower conjecture for shifted prefundamental representations; see Remark~\ref{rem:lemma-L-status} for the dependency chain).
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the entire standard landscape:
+\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the proved standard landscape:
 
 \smallskip
-\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong filtration.}
-If the algebra~$\cA$ carries a filtration satisfying the strong axiom $\mu_r(F^{i_1}\cA,\dots,F^{i_r}\cA) \subset F^{i_1+\cdots+i_r}\cA$, the degree cutoff (Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}) makes continuity and Mittag--Leffler automatic. The completion closure $\CompCl(\Fft)$ then carries a quasi-inverse bar-cobar homotopy equivalence (Corollary~\ref{cor:completion-closure-equivalence}), stable under MC twisting (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc-twisting-closure}), with completed twisting representability (Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-twisting-representability}). For the standard graded families of Proposition~\ref{prop:standard-strong-filtration}, this strong-filtration mechanism applies only after passage to the weight-completed tower: the inverse-limit object is the weight completion $\widehat{\cA}^{\mathrm{wt}}$ rather than the direct sum~$\cA$. Accordingly, the MC4 statement for $V_k(\mathfrak g)$ (non-critical), $\mathrm{Vir}_c$ (all~$c$), $\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak g, f_{\mathrm{prin}})$ (non-critical), and lattice algebras is formulated on their weight completions, not on the uncompleted direct sums.
+\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong completion towers.}
+If the algebra~$\cA$ is a strong completion tower, the degree cutoff
+\textup{(}Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}\textup{)} makes each component

+ (Lemma~\ref{lem:chirhoch-descent}), reducing Theorem~H to Theorem~A's
+ Verdier intertwining.

+\medskip\noindent\textbf{The forced chain A $\Rightarrow$ B $\Rightarrow$ C $\Rightarrow$ D $\Rightarrow$ H.}
+Without Theorem~A, the dual object is only a formal symbol: there is
+no theorem identifying
+$\mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}\barB_X(\cA)$ with a chiral algebra.
+Without Theorem~B, the bar construction can lose the boundary algebra.
+Without Theorem~C, higher-genus deformation theory has no canonical
+polarization. Without Theorem~D, the genus tower has no distinguished
+scalar shadow. Without Theorem~H, the bulk coefficient ring is
+uncontrolled. The unique survivor of all five failures is the
+genus-completed bar differential, first in the ordered $E_1$ theory and
+then in its $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant shadow.
+
+Theorem~A identifies the homotopy Koszul dual on the quadratic Koszul
+lane: for a chiral Koszul pair, the unit and counit are
+quasi-isomorphisms and Verdier duality exchanges the two members of the
+pair. Theorem~B shows that the bar-cobar transform is faithful on the
+Koszul locus: genus~$0$ is unconditional, while the genus-$g \ge 1$
+inversion is proved on the modular pre-Koszul lane and is unconditional
+on the standard CFT-type landscape except integer-spin $\beta\gamma$.
+Once both sides are present, Theorem~C forces the Verdier decomposition
+$\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)$ of the ambient complex;
+the Lagrangian splitting is proved, while the ambient
+shifted-symplectic upgrade remains conditional on perfectness and
+nondegeneracy. Tracing the same object yields Theorem~D: on the
+uniform-weight modular Koszul lane,
+$\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$ at all genera and the free
+energies are the $\hat A$-series; at genus~$1$ this scalar statement is
+unconditional for every family, while at genus $g \ge 2$ multi-weight
+algebras acquire the explicit cross-channel term
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$. Theorem~H identifies the coefficient
+ring of the same universal class: on the Koszul locus,
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n \notin \{0,1,2\}$ and
+\[
+P_\cA(t)
+=

+ P_{\cA}(t)

+ \dim Z(\cA)
+ + \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
+ + \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.

+ Heisenberg gives $1+t+t^2$; generic Virasoro and principal
+ $\mathcal{W}$-algebras give $1+t^2$; generic affine Kac--Moody gives
+ $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg)) \cong \fg$, so no uniform four-dimensional
+ bound is available. Critical level is outside the theorem's regime:

 \noindent\textbf{MC1} (PBW concentration): Bar cohomology of every standard-landscape chiral algebra concentrates in PBW degrees, proved for all standard families by explicit computation.
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): $\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$ exists for every modular Koszul chiral algebra by the bar-intrinsic construction (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}): $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$. The shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of its finite-order projections. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$ (Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}).
+\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): the symmetric, coinvariant statement is
+$\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$, proved by
+the bar-intrinsic construction
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}\textup{)}:
+$\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is Maurer--Cartan because
+$D_\cA^2 = 0$. The primitive ordered statement is
+$\Theta_\cA^{\Eone} := D_\cA^{\Eone} - \dzero \in
+\MC({\gAmod}^{\Eone})$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-mc-element}\textup{)}, and averaging
+recovers~$\Theta_\cA$. The shadow obstruction tower
+$\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of the finite-order projections of this
+coinvariant class. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element
+collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$
+\textup{(}Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}\textup{)}.
 
 \noindent\textbf{MC3} (Thick generation): Proved for all simple types on the evaluation-generated core via multiplicity-free $\ell$-weights~\cite{ChariMoura06} (Theorem~\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}), with DK comparison proved separately (Corollary~\ref{cor:dk23-all-types}, Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types}). For type~$A$, shifted-prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are also proved (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}). DK-4/5 (extension beyond evaluation modules) is downstream.
 
  The proof decomposes into three layers: MC3a (evaluation core, all types, unconditional), MC3b (categorical thick generation, all types, unconditional), and MC3c (full category~$\mathcal{O}$, type~$A$ unconditional; other types conditional on Conjecture~\ref{conj:rank-independence-step2}, the rank-independence lift-and-lower conjecture for shifted prefundamental representations; see Remark~\ref{rem:lemma-L-status} for the dependency chain).
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the entire standard landscape:
+\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the proved standard landscape:
 
 \smallskip
-\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong filtration.}
-If the algebra~$\cA$ carries a filtration satisfying the strong axiom $\mu_r(F^{i_1}\cA,\dots,F^{i_r}\cA) \subset F^{i_1+\cdots+i_r}\cA$, the degree cutoff (Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}) makes continuity and Mittag--Leffler automatic. The completion closure $\CompCl(\Fft)$ then carries a quasi-inverse bar-cobar homotopy equivalence (Corollary~\ref{cor:completion-closure-equivalence}), stable under MC twisting (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc-twisting-closure}), with completed twisting representability (Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-twisting-representability}). For the standard graded families of Proposition~\ref{prop:standard-strong-filtration}, this strong-filtration mechanism applies only after passage to the weight-completed tower: the inverse-limit object is the weight completion $\widehat{\cA}^{\mathrm{wt}}$ rather than the direct sum~$\cA$. Accordingly, the MC4 statement for $V_k(\mathfrak g)$ (non-critical), $\mathrm{Vir}_c$ (all~$c$), $\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak g, f_{\mathrm{prin}})$ (non-critical), and lattice algebras is formulated on their weight completions, not on the uncompleted direct sums.
+\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong completion towers.}
+If the algebra~$\cA$ is a strong completion tower, the degree cutoff
+\textup{(}Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}\textup{)} makes each component
+of the MC equation finite, and the strong completion-tower theorem
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-bar-cobar-strong}\textup{)}
+produces a quasi-inverse completed bar-cobar equivalence, stable under
+MC twisting and completed twisting representability
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:mc-twisting-closure},
+Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-twisting-representability}\textup{)}.
+For the standard graded families of
+Proposition~\ref{prop:standard-strong-filtration}, this mechanism
+applies only after passage to the weight-completed tower: the
+inverse-limit object is the weight completion
+$\widehat{\cA}^{\mathrm{wt}}$ rather than the direct sum~$\cA$.
+Accordingly, the MC4 statement for $V_k(\mathfrak g)$
+\textup{(}non-critical\textup{)}, $\mathrm{Vir}_c$ \textup{(}all~$c$\textup{)},
+$\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak g, f_{\mathrm{prin}})$
+\textup{(}non-critical\textup{)}, and lattice algebras is formulated on
+their weight-completed towers, not on the uncompleted direct sums.
 
 \smallskip

-\medskip\noindent\textbf{The boundary-bulk reconstruction thesis.}
-\emph{The boundary chiral algebra~$\cA$ determines its bulk via the
-chiral derived centre~$\cZ^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$; the
-modular characteristic~$\kappa(\cA)$ is the holographic central charge;
-the shadow obstruction tower~$\{S_r(\cA)\}_{r \ge 2}$ is the holographic
-correction hierarchy; and the Drinfeld double
-$U_\cA = \cA \bowtie \cA^!$ is the universal boundary-bulk algebra.}
-The bar complex computes the boundary $\mapsto$ bulk transform; the
-cobar inverts it on the Koszul locus.
-Each theorem isolates one structural property of this transform.
-Theorem~A constructs the bar-cobar adjunction and its Verdier intertwining
-(Definition~\ref{def:chiral-koszul-pair}).
-Theorem~B inverts the transform: at genus~$0$ via chiral
-Koszulity (Theorem~\ref{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}),
-at genus~$g \ge 1$ via the inductive genus step.
-Theorem~C constrains the Koszul dual pair by a family-specific sum rule:
-$\kappa(\cA) + \kappa(\cA^!) = K_\cA$, where $K = 0$ for affine
-Kac--Moody and free fields and $K = 13$ for Virasoro
-(self-dual at $c = 13$).
-Theorem~D identifies $\kappa$
-as the one-loop coefficient of the bulk partition
-function on the uniform-weight lane; the multi-weight
-correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$ at $g \ge 2$ is a
-separate higher-loop contribution.
-Theorem~H bounds the bulk state space: at generic level on the
-Koszul locus, $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ has total dimension at most four.
+\medskip\noindent\textbf{The forced chain A $\Rightarrow$ B $\Rightarrow$ C $\Rightarrow$ D $\Rightarrow$ H.}
+Without Theorem~A, the dual object is only a formal symbol: there is
+no theorem identifying
+$\mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}\barB_X(\cA)$ with a chiral algebra.
+Without Theorem~B, the bar construction can lose the boundary algebra.
+Without Theorem~C, higher-genus deformation theory has no canonical
+polarization. Without Theorem~D, the genus tower has no distinguished
+scalar shadow. Without Theorem~H, the bulk coefficient ring is
+uncontrolled. The unique survivor of all five failures is the
+genus-completed bar differential, first in the ordered $E_1$ theory and
+then in its $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant shadow.
+
+Theorem~A identifies the homotopy Koszul dual on the quadratic Koszul
+lane: for a chiral Koszul pair, the unit and counit are
+quasi-isomorphisms and Verdier duality exchanges the two members of the
+pair. Theorem~B shows that the bar-cobar transform is faithful on the
+Koszul locus: genus~$0$ is unconditional, while the genus-$g \ge 1$
+inversion is proved on the modular pre-Koszul lane and is unconditional
+on the standard CFT-type landscape except integer-spin $\beta\gamma$.
+Once both sides are present, Theorem~C forces the Verdier decomposition
+$\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)$ of the ambient complex;
+the Lagrangian splitting is proved, while the ambient
+shifted-symplectic upgrade remains conditional on perfectness and
+nondegeneracy. Tracing the same object yields Theorem~D: on the
+uniform-weight modular Koszul lane,
+$\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$ at all genera and the free
+energies are the $\hat A$-series; at genus~$1$ this scalar statement is
+unconditional for every family, while at genus $g \ge 2$ multi-weight
+algebras acquire the explicit cross-channel term
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$. Theorem~H identifies the coefficient
+ring of the same universal class: on the Koszul locus,
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n \notin \{0,1,2\}$ and
+\[
+P_\cA(t)
+=
+\dim Z(\cA)
++ \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
++ \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.
+\]
+For Heisenberg this gives $P(t) = 1+t+t^2$; for generic Virasoro and
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras it gives $P(t) = 1+t^2$; for generic
+affine Kac--Moody one has
+$\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\mathfrak g)) \cong \mathfrak g$, so the coefficient
+ring is still polynomial but need not be four-dimensional.

 \item \emph{Theorem~B} (Bar-cobar inversion,
  Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}).
- On the Koszul locus, the counit
- $\Omega_X \bar{B}_X(\cA) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA$
- is an equivalence; the spectral sequence collapses at~$E_2$.
- Off the locus, the bar-cobar object persists but becomes curved;
- the failure is measured by
- $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_\cA^{(0)}$
- (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}).
- The BRST resolution is complete.
-
- More precisely, there is a spectral sequence with $E_1$ page
- $E_1^{p,q} = H^q(\barB^p_X(\cA))$ converging to $H^{p+q}(\Omega_X \barB_X(\cA))$.
- On the Koszul locus, $E_1^{p,q} = 0$ for $p \neq 1$, so
- $E_2 = E_\infty$, and the comparison map
- $\varepsilon \colon \Omega_X \barB_X(\cA) \to \cA$
- induces an isomorphism on cohomology. This is the categorical
- exponential: $\exp \circ \log = \operatorname{id}$.
+ Without Theorem~B, the bar transform can forget the boundary algebra.
+ Theorem~B removes that failure on the Koszul locus:
+ \[
+ \Omega_X \bar{B}_X(\cA) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA,
+ \]
+ genus by genus, with $E_2$-collapse of the bar-cobar spectral
+ sequence. The genus-$0$ statement is unconditional. The genus-$g \geq 1$
+ statement is proved on the modular pre-Koszul lane and is
+ unconditional on the standard CFT-type landscape except
+ integer-spin $\beta\gamma$. Off the locus, the curved bar-cobar object
+ persists, but the coderived continuation is kept separate from
+ Theorem~B itself. Instant check: for Heisenberg,
+ $\Omega(\barB(\cH_k)) \simeq \cH_k$.
 
 \item \emph{Theorem~C} (Deformation-obstruction complementarity,
  Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}).
- The \emph{center local system} $\mathcal{Z}_\cA$ is the
- local system on $\overline{\cM}_g$ whose fibre over a
- smooth curve $[\Sigma_g]$ is the center
- $Z(\cA|_{\Sigma_g})$ (the commutant of~$\cA$ acting on
- itself via the chiral bracket).
- The \emph{ambient complex}
- $\mathbf{C}_g(\cA) := R\Gamma(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g,
- \mathcal{Z}_\cA)$
- (derived global sections of this local system)
- carries a shifted-symplectic pairing from Verdier duality.
- The Verdier involution decomposes it
- into \emph{complementary Lagrangians}
- $\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)$:
- the $+1$ and $-1$ eigenspaces of the involution
- (Definition~\ref{def:complementarity-complexes}).
- At the cohomological level,
+ The deficiency here is higher-genus ambiguity: without a
+ polarization, one cannot separate deformation from obstruction.
+ Theorem~C says that the Verdier involution is the unique survivor.
+ Writing
+ $\mathbf{C}_g(\cA) := R\Gamma(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g,\mathcal{Z}_\cA)$,
+ one has the homotopy decomposition
+ \[
+ \mathbf{C}_g(\cA)
+ \simeq
+ \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)
+ \]
+ for every genus, and for $g \geq 1$ the induced pairing is perfect
+ with each summand Lagrangian. At the cohomological shadow level,
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:complementarity-intro}
  Q_g(\cA) \oplus Q_g(\cA^!) \cong
  H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, \mathcal{Z}_\cA).
  \end{equation}
- The anomaly of one algebra is the ghost-number violation of
- its dual.
-
- The shifted-symplectic structure is a $(-(3g{-}3))$-shifted symplectic form on the derived moduli of genus-$g$ bar families, in the sense of Pantev--To\"en--Vaqui\'e--Vezzosi~\cite{PTVV13}. The complementary Lagrangians~$Q_g(\cA)$ and~$Q_g(\cA^!)$ are the $\pm 1$ eigenspaces of the Verdier involution~$\sigma = \mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}$ acting on~$\mathbf{C}_g(\cA)$. Their intersection is the center~$Z(\cA)$; the ambient complex is the direct sum.
+ The Verdier-side Lagrangian splitting is proved. The ambient
+ shifted-symplectic upgrade is conditional on perfectness and
+ nondegeneracy. Instant check: for Heisenberg the center local system
+ is constant, so the decomposition is visible at once.
 
-\item \emph{Theorem~D$_{\mathrm{scal}}$} (Scalar modular
- characteristic, Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}).
- A single scalar $\kappa(\cA)$, the genus-$1$ curvature
- coefficient (Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality}), determines the
- entire \emph{scalar modular package}: the collection
- $(\kappa, \{\mathrm{obs}_g\}_{g \ge 1},
- \{F_g\}_{g \ge 1})$ consisting of the modular
- characteristic, its genus-$g$ obstruction classes, and the
- free energies. For algebras whose strong generators have the
- same conformal weight (including all single-generator families),
+\item \emph{Theorem~D$_{\mathrm{scal}}$} (Genus universality,
+ Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality}).
+ The deficiency is scalar drift: genus by genus, the bar tower could
+ produce unrelated numbers. Theorem~D says that on the proved
+ uniform-weight lane they are forced by one scalar
+ $\kappa(\cA)$, determined already at genus~$1$. For algebras whose
+ strong generators have the same conformal weight,
  $\mathrm{obs}_g = \kappa \cdot \lambda_g

+\medskip\noindent\textbf{The forced chain A $\Rightarrow$ B $\Rightarrow$ C $\Rightarrow$ D $\Rightarrow$ H.}
+Without Theorem~A, the dual object is only a formal symbol: there is

+$\mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}\barB_X(\cA)$ with a chiral algebra.
+Without Theorem~B, the bar construction can lose the boundary algebra.

+then in its $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant shadow.

+Theorem~A identifies the homotopy Koszul dual on the quadratic Koszul

+\[
+P_\cA(t)

- The \emph{chiral Hochschild cohomology}
- $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ is the cohomology of the complex

- (coderivations of the bar coalgebra, with differential

- $[0, 2]$ and total dimension at most four.
- The concentration in degrees $\{0,1,2\}$ is the

- For affine Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$ at generic

+ $\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n < 0$ and $n > 2$, and the Hilbert
+ polynomial is
+ \[
+ P_{\cA}(t)
+ =
+ \dim Z(\cA)
+ + \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
+ + \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.
+ \]
+ This is the sharp theorem. The specializations are family-sensitive:
+ Heisenberg gives $1+t+t^2$; generic Virasoro and principal
+ $\mathcal{W}$-algebras give $1+t^2$; generic affine Kac--Moody gives
+ $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg)) \cong \fg$, so no uniform four-dimensional
+ bound is available. Critical level is outside the theorem's regime:
+ there the Beilinson--Drinfeld comparison yields an unbounded oper-type
+ center. The primitive step is the descent lemma

 \noindent\textbf{MC1} (PBW concentration): Bar cohomology of every standard-landscape chiral algebra concentrates in PBW degrees, proved for all standard families by explicit computation.
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): $\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$ exists for every modular Koszul chiral algebra by the bar-intrinsic construction (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}): $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$. The shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of its finite-order projections. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$ (Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}).
+\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): the symmetric, coinvariant statement is
+$\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$, proved by
+the bar-intrinsic construction
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}\textup{)}:
+$\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is Maurer--Cartan because
+$D_\cA^2 = 0$. The primitive ordered statement is
+$\Theta_\cA^{\Eone} := D_\cA^{\Eone} - \dzero \in
+\MC({\gAmod}^{\Eone})$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-mc-element}\textup{)}, and averaging
+recovers~$\Theta_\cA$. The shadow obstruction tower
+$\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of the finite-order projections of this
+coinvariant class. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element
+collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$
+\textup{(}Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}\textup{)}.
 
 \noindent\textbf{MC3} (Thick generation): Proved for all simple types on the evaluation-generated core via multiplicity-free $\ell$-weights~\cite{ChariMoura06} (Theorem~\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}), with DK comparison proved separately (Corollary~\ref{cor:dk23-all-types}, Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types}). For type~$A$, shifted-prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are also proved (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}). DK-4/5 (extension beyond evaluation modules) is downstream.
 
  The proof decomposes into three layers: MC3a (evaluation core, all types, unconditional), MC3b (categorical thick generation, all types, unconditional), and MC3c (full category~$\mathcal{O}$, type~$A$ unconditional; other types conditional on Conjecture~\ref{conj:rank-independence-step2}, the rank-independence lift-and-lower conjecture for shifted prefundamental representations; see Remark~\ref{rem:lemma-L-status} for the dependency chain).
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the entire standard landscape:
+\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the proved standard landscape:
 
 \smallskip
-\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong filtration.}
-If the algebra~$\cA$ carries a filtration satisfying the strong axiom $\mu_r(F^{i_1}\cA,\dots,F^{i_r}\cA) \subset F^{i_1+\cdots+i_r}\cA$, the degree cutoff (Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}) makes continuity and Mittag--Leffler automatic. The completion closure $\CompCl(\Fft)$ then carries a quasi-inverse bar-cobar homotopy equivalence (Corollary~\ref{cor:completion-closure-equivalence}), stable under MC twisting (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc-twisting-closure}), with completed twisting representability (Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-twisting-representability}). For the standard graded families of Proposition~\ref{prop:standard-strong-filtration}, this strong-filtration mechanism applies only after passage to the weight-completed tower: the inverse-limit object is the weight completion $\widehat{\cA}^{\mathrm{wt}}$ rather than the direct sum~$\cA$. Accordingly, the MC4 statement for $V_k(\mathfrak g)$ (non-critical), $\mathrm{Vir}_c$ (all~$c$), $\mathcal{W}^k(\mathfrak g, f_{\mathrm{prin}})$ (non-critical), and lattice algebras is formulated on their weight completions, not on the uncompleted direct sums.
+\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong completion towers.}
+If the algebra~$\cA$ is a strong completion tower, the degree cutoff
+\textup{(}Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}\textup{)} makes each component
+of the MC equation finite, and the strong completion-tower theorem
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-bar-cobar-strong}\textup{)}
+produces a quasi-inverse completed bar-cobar equivalence, stable under
+MC twisting and completed twisting representability
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:mc-twisting-closure},
+Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-twisting-representability}\textup{)}.

-the shadow obstruction tower~$\{S_r(\cA)\}_{r \ge 2}$ is the holographic

-$U_\cA = \cA \bowtie \cA^!$ is the universal boundary-bulk algebra.}

-cobar inverts it on the Koszul locus.

-Theorem~A constructs the bar-cobar adjunction and its Verdier intertwining

-(self-dual at $c = 13$).

-separate higher-loop contribution.

+\medskip\noindent\textbf{The forced chain A $\Rightarrow$ B $\Rightarrow$ C $\Rightarrow$ D $\Rightarrow$ H.}
+Without Theorem~A, the dual object is only a formal symbol: there is
+no theorem identifying
+$\mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}\barB_X(\cA)$ with a chiral algebra.
+Without Theorem~B, the bar construction can lose the boundary algebra.
+Without Theorem~C, higher-genus deformation theory has no canonical
+polarization. Without Theorem~D, the genus tower has no distinguished
+scalar shadow. Without Theorem~H, the bulk coefficient ring is
+uncontrolled. The unique survivor of all five failures is the
+genus-completed bar differential, first in the ordered $E_1$ theory and
+then in its $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant shadow.
+
+Theorem~A identifies the homotopy Koszul dual on the quadratic Koszul
+lane: for a chiral Koszul pair, the unit and counit are
+quasi-isomorphisms and Verdier duality exchanges the two members of the
+pair. Theorem~B shows that the bar-cobar transform is faithful on the

+on the standard CFT-type landscape except integer-spin $\beta\gamma$.

+$\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)$ of the ambient complex;

+shifted-symplectic upgrade remains conditional on perfectness and

+uniform-weight modular Koszul lane,
+$\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$ at all genera and the free

+algebras acquire the explicit cross-channel term
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$. Theorem~H identifies the coefficient

+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n \notin \{0,1,2\}$ and
+\[
+P_\cA(t)
+=
+\dim Z(\cA)
++ \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
++ \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.

+For Heisenberg this gives $P(t) = 1+t+t^2$; for generic Virasoro and

+affine Kac--Moody one has
+$\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\mathfrak g)) \cong \mathfrak g$, so the coefficient
+ring is still polynomial but need not be four-dimensional.

- $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ is the cohomology of the complex

- The finite-dimensional bound is the tightness of

+ $\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n < 0$ and $n > 2$, and the Hilbert

+ \[
+ P_{\cA}(t)
+ =
+ \dim Z(\cA)
+ + \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
+ + \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.
+ \]
+ This is the sharp theorem. The specializations are family-sensitive:
+ Heisenberg gives $1+t+t^2$; generic Virasoro and principal
+ $\mathcal{W}$-algebras give $1+t^2$; generic affine Kac--Moody gives
+ $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg)) \cong \fg$, so no uniform four-dimensional
+ bound is available. Critical level is outside the theorem's regime:
+ there the Beilinson--Drinfeld comparison yields an unbounded oper-type

**Organizing the report structure**

I'm considering whether macro expansions are necessary. I think I should mention a previous build failure due to an undefined control sequence later in the manuscript, not in the introduction. I’ll run a search command to check for outputs related to `\Omega/z`. I need to produce a final report with sections for each theorem touched, using the specified format. I’m thinking I should start with a brief introduction followed by the report. I need to mention that a build check on `main.tex` revealed unrelated pre-existing undefined control sequences and unresolved references, but our intro changes have passed through checks. I’ll include a link to the exact file changed, which is `chapters/theory/introduction.tex`. I want to keep the report concise, maybe using flat bullets for clarity without nesting. The report should cover verification and finalize with required details.
thinking
**Summarizing theorems**

I think I should mention the strongest forms of theorems in a plain summary. It's important to present these key aspects clearly, so everyone can understand their significance without getting lost in technical jargon. I want to be sure that my summary is accessible while still highlighting the essential details of these theorems. This way, I can ensure that the user gets the information they need in a straightforward manner.
thinking

I’m thinking about how to structure information without using nested bullets. It seems I can use bold headings as section lines, like “Theorem A,” followed by flat bullet points underneath. That works for creating a hierarchy but keeps sections distinct. Okay, I feel good about that! I’ll focus on crafting concise statements to keep everything clear and organized. This should help present the information effectively!

Edited [chapters/theory/introduction.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/introduction.tex:338) to rebuild the theorem-announcement lane around the strongest live theorem surfaces. The opening now makes A→B→C→D→H a forced chain, the Heisenberg subsection is recast as the instant check for each theorem, the general A/B/C/D/H announcements match the body theorems, the conjectural boundary-bulk summary no longer overstates C or H, the ordered/E₁ residue now cites [e1_modular_koszul.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/e1_modular_koszul.tex:291), and the MC2/MC4 recap now distinguishes coinvariant MC2 from the ordered lift and states MC4 on weight-completed towers.

Verification: re-read the modified regions at [introduction.tex:338](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/introduction.tex:338), [introduction.tex:442](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/introduction.tex:442), [introduction.tex:552](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/introduction.tex:552), [introduction.tex:761](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/introduction.tex:761), and [introduction.tex:2057](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/introduction.tex:2057). `git diff --check` is clean. AP132 and AP29 grep checks are clean on this file. AP126 grep found only level-prefixed `k\,\Omega/z` occurrences, not bare `\Omega/z`. A `latexmk` run did not give a clean manuscript build because of a pre-existing unrelated undefined control sequence `\E` later in the manuscript (`main.log`, input line 1890), outside the edited introduction surface.

## Platonic Rectification Report

**Theorem A**
- BEFORE: Announced as proved bar-cobar adjunction/Verdier intertwining, but without a sharp deficiency opening or a clean statement of what is actually recovered.
- ISSUE: The introduction did not make clear that A is the theorem that turns the formal Verdier-dual symbol into a proved chiral dual.
- ACTION: STRENGTHENED
- AFTER: Announced as the quadratic Koszul-locus theorem identifying the homotopy Koszul dual via unit/counit quasi-isomorphisms and Verdier exchange, with the Heisenberg residue as the instant check.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: none in the introduction surface.

**Theorem B**
- BEFORE: Announced as if higher-genus inversion simply followed from induction, with no explicit scope boundary.
- ISSUE: It omitted the modular pre-Koszul hypothesis and the standard-landscape exception structure, which risked overclaiming genus-$g\ge1$ inversion.
- ACTION: STRENGTHENED
- AFTER: Announced as genus-$0$ unconditional, genus-$g\ge1$ proved on the modular pre-Koszul lane, unconditional on the standard CFT-type landscape except integer-spin `\beta\gamma`, with off-locus coderived continuation kept separate.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: the theorem itself still stops at the proved modular-pre-Koszul lane.

**Theorem C**
- BEFORE: Collapsed in the introduction to a family-specific scalar sum rule.
- ISSUE: That misstated the theorem’s unique survivor: the proved result is the Verdier/Lagrangian splitting, while scalar conductors are only shadows.
- ACTION: STRENGTHENED
- AFTER: Announced as the homotopy and cohomological decomposition `\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA)\oplus\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)`, with the ambient shifted-symplectic upgrade explicitly marked conditional on perfectness/nondegeneracy.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: the ambient shifted-symplectic upgrade remains conditional, exactly as stated.

**Theorem D**
- BEFORE: Mixed scalar-lane truth with language that could be read as all-weight/all-genus universality.
- ISSUE: The introduction did not cleanly separate uniform-weight all-genera, genus-$1$ all-weight, and multi-weight cross-channel corrections.
- ACTION: STRENGTHENED
- AFTER: Announced as genus universality on the uniform-weight modular Koszul lane, genus-$1$ unconditional for every family, and multi-weight genus-$g\ge2$ corrected by `\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}`; the Heisenberg `F_1=k/24` and `\hat A` series remain the instant check.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: none beyond the theorem’s stated uniform-weight boundary.

**Theorem H**
- BEFORE: Announced with the false claim that the bulk state space has total dimension at most four.
- ISSUE: This contradicted the affine generic case `\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\mathfrak g))\cong\mathfrak g`.
- ACTION: FILLED
- AFTER: Announced by the actual sharp theorem: amplitude `[0,2]` plus Hilbert polynomial `P_\cA(t)=\dim Z(\cA)+\dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)t+\dim Z(\cA^!)t^2`, with family-sensitive specializations and explicit critical-level exclusion.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: none in the announcement; critical level remains outside Theorem H, as stated.

**MC2**
- BEFORE: The ordered/E₁ primitive was liable to be read as if it were proved by the symmetric coinvariant MC2 theorem alone.
- ISSUE: Ordered and coinvariant MC2 were not clearly separated.
- ACTION: FILLED
- AFTER: The introduction now states the symmetric coinvariant theorem via `thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic` and the ordered primitive via `thm:e1-mc-element`, with averaging explicitly linking them.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: none.

**MC4**
- BEFORE: The recap could be read as applying directly to uncompleted standard-family algebras.
- ISSUE: The weight-completion qualifier was not load-bearing enough.
- ACTION: FILLED
- AFTER: The introduction now states MC4 on strong completion towers, and for standard graded families explicitly on the weight-completed towers `\widehat{\cA}^{\mathrm{wt}}`, not on the direct sums; the weight-cutoff lane and finite-window residue are also stated.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: the remaining example-specific finite-window stabilization and H-level target identification are still open, and are stated as such.

-\medskip\noindent\textbf{The boundary-bulk reconstruction thesis.}
-\emph{The boundary chiral algebra~$\cA$ determines its bulk via the
-chiral derived centre~$\cZ^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$; the
-modular characteristic~$\kappa(\cA)$ is the holographic central charge;
-the shadow obstruction tower~$\{S_r(\cA)\}_{r \ge 2}$ is the holographic
-correction hierarchy; and the Drinfeld double
-$U_\cA = \cA \bowtie \cA^!$ is the universal boundary-bulk algebra.}
-The bar complex computes the boundary $\mapsto$ bulk transform; the
-cobar inverts it on the Koszul locus.
-Each theorem isolates one structural property of this transform.
-Theorem~A constructs the bar-cobar adjunction and its Verdier intertwining
-(Definition~\ref{def:chiral-koszul-pair}).
-Theorem~B inverts the transform: at genus~$0$ via chiral
-Koszulity (Theorem~\ref{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}),
-at genus~$g \ge 1$ via the inductive genus step.
-Theorem~C constrains the Koszul dual pair by a family-specific sum rule:
-$\kappa(\cA) + \kappa(\cA^!) = K_\cA$, where $K = 0$ for affine
-Kac--Moody and free fields and $K = 13$ for Virasoro
-(self-dual at $c = 13$).
-Theorem~D identifies $\kappa$
-as the one-loop coefficient of the bulk partition
-function on the uniform-weight lane; the multi-weight
-correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$ at $g \ge 2$ is a
-separate higher-loop contribution.
-Theorem~H bounds the bulk state space: at generic level on the
-Koszul locus, $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ has total dimension at most four.
+\medskip\noindent\textbf{The forced chain A $\Rightarrow$ B $\Rightarrow$ C $\Rightarrow$ D $\Rightarrow$ H.}
+Without Theorem~A, the dual object is only a formal symbol: there is
+no theorem identifying
+$\mathbb{D}_{\operatorname{Ran}}\barB_X(\cA)$ with a chiral algebra.
+Without Theorem~B, the bar construction can lose the boundary algebra.
+Without Theorem~C, higher-genus deformation theory has no canonical
+polarization. Without Theorem~D, the genus tower has no distinguished
+scalar shadow. Without Theorem~H, the bulk coefficient ring is
+uncontrolled. The unique survivor of all five failures is the
+genus-completed bar differential, first in the ordered $E_1$ theory and
+then in its $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant shadow.
+
+Theorem~A identifies the homotopy Koszul dual on the quadratic Koszul
+lane: for a chiral Koszul pair, the unit and counit are
+quasi-isomorphisms and Verdier duality exchanges the two members of the
+pair. Theorem~B shows that the bar-cobar transform is faithful on the
+Koszul locus: genus~$0$ is unconditional, while the genus-$g \ge 1$
+inversion is proved on the modular pre-Koszul lane and is unconditional
+on the standard CFT-type landscape except integer-spin $\beta\gamma$.
+Once both sides are present, Theorem~C forces the Verdier decomposition
+$\mathbf{Q}_g(\cA) \oplus \mathbf{Q}_g(\cA^!)$ of the ambient complex;
+the Lagrangian splitting is proved, while the ambient
+shifted-symplectic upgrade remains conditional on perfectness and
+nondegeneracy. Tracing the same object yields Theorem~D: on the
+uniform-weight modular Koszul lane,
+$\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$ at all genera and the free
+energies are the $\hat A$-series; at genus~$1$ this scalar statement is
+unconditional for every family, while at genus $g \ge 2$ multi-weight
+algebras acquire the explicit cross-channel term
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$. Theorem~H identifies the coefficient
+ring of the same universal class: on the Koszul locus,
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA) = 0$ for $n \notin \{0,1,2\}$ and
+\[
+P_\cA(t)
+=
+\dim Z(\cA)
++ \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
++ \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.
+\]
+For Heisenberg this gives $P(t) = 1+t+t^2$; for generic Virasoro and
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras it gives $P(t) = 1+t^2$; for generic
+affine Kac--Moody one has
+$\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\mathfrak g)) \cong \mathfrak g$, so the coefficient

+ P_{\cA}(t)

+ \dim Z(\cA)
+ + \dim \ChirHoch^1(\cA)\, t
+ + \dim Z(\cA^!)\, t^2.

+ Heisenberg gives $1+t+t^2$; generic Virasoro and principal

 \noindent\textbf{MC1} (PBW concentration): Bar cohomology of every standard-landscape chiral algebra concentrates in PBW degrees, proved for all standard families by explicit computation.
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): $\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$ exists for every modular Koszul chiral algebra by the bar-intrinsic construction (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}): $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$. The shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of its finite-order projections. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$ (Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}).
+\noindent\textbf{MC2} (Universal MC class): the symmetric, coinvariant statement is
+$\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\Defcyc(\cA) \widehat{\otimes} \Gmod)$, proved by
+the bar-intrinsic construction
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}\textup{)}:
+$\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - \dzero$ is Maurer--Cartan because
+$D_\cA^2 = 0$. The primitive ordered statement is
+$\Theta_\cA^{\Eone} := D_\cA^{\Eone} - \dzero \in
+\MC({\gAmod}^{\Eone})$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-mc-element}\textup{)}, and averaging
+recovers~$\Theta_\cA$. The shadow obstruction tower
+$\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ consists of the finite-order projections of this
+coinvariant class. When $\dim H^2_{\mathrm{cyc}} = 1$, the MC element
+collapses to $\Theta_\cA = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \eta \otimes \Lambda$
+\textup{(}Corollary~\ref{cor:scalar-saturation}\textup{)}.
 
 \noindent\textbf{MC3} (Thick generation): Proved for all simple types on the evaluation-generated core via multiplicity-free $\ell$-weights~\cite{ChariMoura06} (Theorem~\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}), with DK comparison proved separately (Corollary~\ref{cor:dk23-all-types}, Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types}). For type~$A$, shifted-prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are also proved (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}). DK-4/5 (extension beyond evaluation modules) is downstream.
 
  The proof decomposes into three layers: MC3a (evaluation core, all types, unconditional), MC3b (categorical thick generation, all types, unconditional), and MC3c (full category~$\mathcal{O}$, type~$A$ unconditional; other types conditional on Conjecture~\ref{conj:rank-independence-step2}, the rank-independence lift-and-lower conjecture for shifted prefundamental representations; see Remark~\ref{rem:lemma-L-status} for the dependency chain).
 
-\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the entire standard landscape:
+\noindent\textbf{MC4} ($\mathcal{W}_\infty$ closure and completed bar-cobar): The completed bar-cobar problem asks whether finite-stage Koszul duality passes to inverse limits. Two complementary mechanisms cover the proved standard landscape:
 
 \smallskip
-\noindent\emph{Mechanism~1: strong filtration.}
-If the algebra~$\cA$ carries a filtration satisfying the strong axiom $\mu_r(F^{i_1}\cA,\dots,F^{i_r}\cA) \subset F^{i_1+\cdots+i_r}\cA$, the degree cutoff (Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}) makes continuity and Mittag--Leffler automatic. The completion closure $\CompCl(\Fft)$ then carries a quasi-inverse bar-cobar homotopy equivalence (Corollary~\ref{cor:completion-closure-equivalence}), stable under MC twisting (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc-twisting-closure}), with completed twisting representability (Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-twisting-representability}).
